message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should exit non-zero when a claim has more damage entries of a type than the policy covers"
Expected: CLI exits non-zero when damage entries of a type exceed insured count (e.g. 2 sword damages, only 1 sword insured)
Current error: CLI exits 0
Implementation file: src/cli.ts

In the CLI, when validating a claim, count damages per type and policy items per type. If damages per type > policy items per type for any type, fail.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text:
All 34 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added damage-count-per-type validation in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_04-46-00_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/cli.ts`. Built two maps counting policy items and damages per type, then failed with non-zero exit and matching stderr message ("more damage entries of type ... than the policy covers") when damage count exceeds policy count for any type.
**Result**: All tests now pass (34 passing, 1 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal addition inside the existing `if (scenario.command === "claim")` block. Reused the already-derived `items` and `damages` arrays. The error message satisfies the regex `/more damage entries.*than.*polic/i` from the test. No other behavior changed; quote flow and other claim validations remain untouched.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-15_04-46-00_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/cli.ts
